    36	RZ_IPI int bb_cmpaddr(const void *_a, const void *_b, void *user) {
    37	        const RzAnalysisBlock *a = _a, *b = _b;
    38	        return (a->addr > b->addr) - (a->addr < b->addr);
    39	}

    41	RZ_IPI int fcn_cmpaddr(const void *_a, const void *_b, void *user) {
    42	        const RzAnalysisFunction *a = _a, *b = _b;
    43	        return (a->addr > b->addr) - (a->addr < b->addr);

    46	static char *getFunctionName(RzCore *core, ut64 addr) {
    47	        RzBinFile *bf = rz_bin_cur(core->bin);
    48	
    49	        RzBinSymbol *sym = bf && bf->o ? rz_bin_object_find_method_by_vaddr(bf->o, addr) : NULL;
    50	        if (sym && sym->classname && sym->name) {
    51	                return rz_str_newf("method.%s.%s", sym->classname, sym->name);
    52	        }
    53	
    54	        RzFlagItem *flag = rz_core_flag_get_by_spaces(core->flags, addr);
    55	        return (flag && flag->name) ? rz_str_dup(flag->name) : NULL;
    56	}
    57	
    58	static char *getFunctionNamePrefix(RzCore *core, ut64 off, const char *name) {
    59	        if (rz_reg_get(core->analysis->reg, name, -1)) {
    60	                return rz_str_newf("%s.%08" PFMT64x, "fcn", off);
    61	        }
    62	        return rz_str_dup(name);
    63	}
    64	
    65	static bool find_string_at(RzCore *core, RzBinObject *bobj, ut64 pointer, char **string, size_t *length, RzStrEnc *encoding) {
    66	        RzBin *bin = core->bin;
    67	        ut8 buffer[512] = { 0 };
    68	        bool ret = false;
    69	        RzDetectedString *detected = NULL;
    70	
    71	        RzList *strings = rz_list_newf((RzListFree)rz_detected_string_free);
    72	        if (!strings) {
    73	                return false;
    74	        }
    75	
    76	        RzStrEnc strenc = bin->str_search_cfg.string_encoding;
    77	        RzUtilStrScanOptions scan_opt = {
    78	                .max_str_length = sizeof(buffer),
    79	                .min_str_length = bin->str_search_cfg.min_length,
    80	                .prefer_big_endian = core->analysis->big_endian,
    81	                .check_ascii_freq = bin->str_search_cfg.check_ascii_freq,
    82	        };
    83	
    84	        rz_io_pread_at(core->io, pointer, buffer, sizeof(buffer));
    85	        if (rz_scan_strings_raw(buffer, strings, &scan_opt, 0, sizeof(buffer), strenc) < 1 ||
    86	                !(detected = rz_list_first_val(strings)) ||
    87	                // ignore any address that is not address 0
    88	                // because we only want strings starting at 0
    89	                detected->addr) {
    90	                goto end;
    91	        }
    92	
    93	        if (string) {
    94	                *string = detected->string;
    95	                detected->string = NULL;
    96	        }
    97	        if (length) {
    98	                *length = detected->size;
    99	        }
   100	        if (encoding) {
   101	                *encoding = detected->encoding;
   102	        }
   103	        ret = true;
   104	
   105	end:
   106	        rz_list_free(strings);
   107	        return ret;
   108	}
   109	
   110	RZ_IPI bool rz_core_get_string_at(RzCore *core, ut64 address, char **string, size_t *length, RzStrEnc *encoding, bool can_search) {
   111	        ut8 tmp64[32] = { 0 };
   112	        ut64 pointer = UT64_MAX, paddress = 0;
   113	        RzIOMap *map = NULL;
   114	        RzBinString *bstr = NULL;
   115	        RzBinObject *bobj = rz_bin_cur_object(core->bin);
   116	        if (!bobj) {
   117	                return false;
   118	        }
   119	
   120	        map = rz_io_map_get(core->io, address);
   121	        if (map && (map->perm & RZ_PERM_RX) != RZ_PERM_RX && (map->perm & RZ_PERM_X)) {
   122	                return false;
   123	        }
   124	
   125	        if (core->io->va && (paddress = rz_io_v2p(core->io, address)) != UT64_MAX) {
   126	                address = paddress;
   127	        }
   128	
   129	        if (rz_io_read_at_mapped(core->io, address, tmp64, sizeof(tmp64))) {
   130	                // checks if is a pointer to a string structure
   131	                pointer = rz_read_ble(tmp64, core->analysis->big_endian, core->analysis->bits);
   132	        }
   133	
   134	        bstr = rz_bin_object_get_string_at(bobj, address, false);
   135	        if (!bstr) {
   136	                if (!pointer) {
   137	                        // maybe is a cstring
   138	                        // usually a cstring has a header set to 0, then the length and then the actual pointer to the string.
   139	                        ut32 n_bytes = core->analysis->bits / 8;
   140	                        ut64 clength = rz_read_ble(&tmp64[n_bytes], core->analysis->big_endian, core->analysis->bits);
   141	                        if (clength < 1000) {
   142	                                pointer = rz_read_ble(tmp64, core->analysis->big_endian, core->analysis->bits);
   143	                        }
   144	                }
   145	
   146	                if (core->io->va && (paddress = rz_io_v2p(core->io, pointer)) != UT64_MAX) {
   147	                        pointer = paddress;
   148	                }
   149	
   150	                bstr = rz_bin_object_get_string_at(bobj, pointer, false);
   151	                if (!bstr) {
   152	                        return can_search && (find_string_at(core, bobj, address, string, length, encoding) || find_string_at(core, bobj, pointer, string, length, encoding));
   153	                }
   154	        }
   155	
   156	        if (string) {
   157	                *string = rz_str_ndup(bstr->string, bstr->length);
   158	        }
   159	        if (length) {
   160	                *length = bstr->size;
   161	        }
   162	        if (encoding) {
   163	                *encoding = bstr->type;
   164	        }
   165	        return true;
   166	}
   167	
   168	/* returns the RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_* of the address 'addr' */
   169	RZ_API ut64 rz_core_analysis_address(RzCore *core, ut64 addr) {
   170	        ut64 types = 0;
   171	        RzRegSet *rs = NULL;
   172	        if (!core) {
   173	                return 0;
   174	        }
   175	        rs = rz_reg_regset_get(core->analysis->reg, RZ_REG_TYPE_GPR);
   176	        if (rs) {
   177	                RzRegItem *r;
   178	                RzListIter *iter;
   179	                rz_list_foreach (rs->regs, iter, r) {
   180	                        if (r->type == RZ_REG_TYPE_GPR) {
   181	                                ut64 val = rz_reg_getv(core->analysis->reg, r->name);
   182	                                if (addr == val) {
   183	                                        types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_REG;
   184	                                        break;
   185	                                }
   186	                        }
   187	                }
   188	        }
   189	        if (rz_flag_get_i(core->flags, addr)) {
   190	                types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_FLAG;
   191	        }
   192	        if (rz_analysis_get_fcn_in(core->analysis, addr, 0)) {
   193	                types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_FUNC;
   194	        }
   195	        // check registers
   196	        if (rz_core_is_debug(core)) {
   197	                RzDebugMap *map;
   198	                RzListIter *iter;
   199	                // use 'dm'
   200	                // XXX: this line makes rz debugging MUCH slower
   201	                // rz_debug_map_sync (core->dbg);
   202	                rz_list_foreach (core->dbg->maps, iter, map) {
   203	                        if (addr >= map->addr && addr < map->addr_end) {
   204	                                if (map->name && map->name[0] == '/') {
   205	                                        if (core->io && core->io->desc &&
   206	                                                core->io->desc->name &&
   207	                                                !strcmp(map->name,
   208	                                                        core->io->desc->name)) {
   209	                                                types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_PROGRAM;
   210	                                        } else {
   211	                                                types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_LIBRARY;
   212	                                        }
   213	                                }
   214	                                if (map->perm & RZ_PERM_X) {
   215	                                        types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_EXEC;
   216	                                }
   217	                                if (map->perm & RZ_PERM_R) {
   218	                                        types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_READ;
   219	                                }
   220	                                if (map->perm & RZ_PERM_W) {
   221	                                        types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_WRITE;
   222	                                }
   223	                                // find function
   224	                                if (map->name && strstr(map->name, "heap")) {
   225	                                        types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_HEAP;
   226	                                }
   227	                                if (map->name && strstr(map->name, "stack")) {
   228	                                        types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_STACK;
   229	                                }
   230	                                break;
   231	                        }
   232	                }
   233	        } else {
   234	                int _perm = -1;
   235	                if (core->io) {
   236	                        // sections
   237	                        void **it;
   238	                        RzPVector *maps = rz_io_maps(core->io);
   239	                        rz_pvector_foreach (maps, it) {
   240	                                RzIOMap *s = *it;
   241	                                if (addr >= s->itv.addr && addr < (s->itv.addr + s->itv.size)) {
   242	                                        // sections overlap, so we want to get the one with lower perms
   243	                                        _perm = (_perm != -1) ? RZ_MIN(_perm, s->perm) : s->perm;
   244	                                        // TODO: we should identify which maps come from the program or other
   245	                                        // types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_PROGRAM;
   246	                                        // find function those sections should be created by hand or esil init
   247	                                        if (s->name && strstr(s->name, "heap")) {
   248	                                                types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_HEAP;
   249	                                        }
   250	                                        if (s->name && strstr(s->name, "stack")) {
   251	                                                types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_STACK;
   252	                                        }
   253	                                }
   254	                        }
   255	                }
   256	                if (_perm != -1) {
   257	                        if (_perm & RZ_PERM_X) {
   258	                                types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_EXEC;
   259	                        }
   260	                        if (_perm & RZ_PERM_R) {
   261	                                types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_READ;
   262	                        }
   263	                        if (_perm & RZ_PERM_W) {
   264	                                types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_WRITE;
   265	                        }
   266	                }
   267	        }
   268	
   269	        // check if it's ascii
   270	        if (addr != 0) {
   271	                int not_ascii = 0;
   272	                int i, failed_sequence, dir, on;
   273	                for (i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
   274	                        ut8 n = (addr >> (i * 8)) & 0xff;
   275	                        if (n && !IS_PRINTABLE(n)) {
   276	                                not_ascii = 1;
   277	                        }
   278	                }
   279	                if (!not_ascii) {
   280	                        types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_ASCII;
   281	                }
   282	                failed_sequence = 0;
   283	                dir = on = -1;
   284	                for (i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
   285	                        ut8 n = (addr >> (i * 8)) & 0xff;
   286	                        if (on != -1) {
   287	                                if (dir == -1) {
   288	                                        dir = (n > on) ? 1 : -1;
   289	                                }
   290	                                if (n == on + dir) {
   291	                                        // ok
   292	                                } else {
   293	                                        failed_sequence = 1;
   294	                                        break;
   295	                                }
   296	                        }
   297	                        on = n;
   298	                }
   299	                if (!failed_sequence) {
   300	                        types |= RZ_ANALYSIS_ADDR_TYPE_SEQUENCE;
   301	                }
   302	        }
   303	        return types;
   304	}
   305	
   306	RZ_IPI void rz_core_analysis_bbs_asciiart(RzCore *core, RzAnalysisFunction *fcn) {
   307	        RzList *flist = rz_list_newf((RzListFree)rz_debug_listinfo_free);
   308	        if (!flist) {
   309	                return;
   310	        }
   311	
   312	        RzAnalysisBlock *b;
   313	        void **iter;
   314	        rz_pvector_foreach (fcn->bbs, iter) {
   315	                b = (RzAnalysisBlock *)*iter;
   316	                RzInterval inter = (RzInterval){ b->addr, b->size };
   317	                RzDbgListInfo *info = rz_debug_listinfo_new(NULL, inter, inter, -1, NULL);
   318	                if (!info) {
   319	                        break;
   320	                }
   321	                rz_list_append(flist, info);
   322	        }
   323	        RzTable *table = rz_core_table(core);
   324	        rz_core_debug_listinfo_to_table(table, flist, core->offset, core->blocksize,
   325	                rz_cons_get_size(NULL), rz_config_get_i(core->config, "scr.color"));
   326	        rz_cons_printf("\n%s\n", rz_table_tostring(table));
   327	        rz_table_free(table);
   328	        rz_list_free(flist);
   329	}
   330	
   331	RZ_IPI void rz_core_analysis_fcn_returns(RzCore *core, RzAnalysisFunction *fcn) {
   332	        RzAnalysisBlock *b;
   333	        void **iter;
   334	        rz_pvector_foreach (fcn->bbs, iter) {
   335	                b = (RzAnalysisBlock *)*iter;
   336	                if (b->jump == UT64_MAX) {
   337	                        ut64 retaddr = rz_analysis_block_get_op_addr(b, b->ninstr - 1);
   338	                        if (retaddr == UT64_MAX) {
   339	                                break;
   340	                        }
   341	
   342	                        rz_cons_printf("0x%08" PFMT64x "\n", retaddr);
   343	                }
   344	        }
   345	}
   346	
   347	static int casecmp(const void *_a, const void *_b, void *user) {
   348	        const RzAnalysisCaseOp *a = _a;
   349	        const RzAnalysisCaseOp *b = _b;
   350	        return a->addr != b->addr;
   351	}
   352	
   353	static ut64 __opaddr(RzAnalysisBlock *b, ut64 addr) {
   354	        int i;
   355	        if (addr >= b->addr && addr < (b->addr + b->size)) {
   356	                for (i = 0; i < b->ninstr; i++) {
   357	                        ut64 aa = rz_analysis_block_get_op_addr(b, i);
   358	                        ut64 ab = rz_analysis_block_get_op_addr(b, i + 1);
   359	                        if (addr >= aa && addr < ab) {
   360	                                return aa;
   361	                        }
   362	                }
   363	        }
   364	        return UT64_MAX;
   365	}
   366	
   367	static void bb_info_print(RzCore *core, RzAnalysisFunction *fcn, RzAnalysisBlock *bb,
   368	        ut64 addr, RzOutputMode mode, PJ *pj, RzTable *t) {
   369	        RzDebugTracepoint *tp = NULL;
   370	        int outputs = (bb->jump != UT64_MAX) + (bb->fail != UT64_MAX);
   371	        int inputs = 0;
   372	
   373	        void **iter;
   374	        RzAnalysisBlock *bb2;
   375	        rz_pvector_foreach (fcn->bbs, iter) {
   376	                bb2 = (RzAnalysisBlock *)*iter;
   377	                inputs += (bb2->jump == bb->addr) + (bb2->fail == bb->addr);
   378	        }
   379	        if (bb->switch_op) {
   380	                RzList *unique_cases = rz_list_uniq(bb->switch_op->cases, casecmp, NULL);
   381	                outputs += rz_list_length(unique_cases);
   382	                rz_list_free(unique_cases);
   383	        }
   384	        ut64 opaddr = __opaddr(bb, addr);
   385	
   386	        switch (mode) {
   387	        case RZ_OUTPUT_MODE_STANDARD:
   388	                tp = rz_debug_trace_get(core->dbg, bb->addr);
   389	                rz_cons_printf("0x%08" PFMT64x " 0x%08" PFMT64x " %02X:%04X %" PFMT64d,
   390	                        bb->addr, bb->addr + bb->size,
   391	                        tp ? tp->times : 0, tp ? tp->count : 0,
   392	                        bb->size);
   393	                if (bb->jump != UT64_MAX) {
   394	                        rz_cons_printf(" j 0x%08" PFMT64x, bb->jump);
   395	                }
   396	                if (bb->fail != UT64_MAX) {
   397	                        rz_cons_printf(" f 0x%08" PFMT64x, bb->fail);
   398	                }
   399	                if (bb->switch_op) {
   400	                        RzAnalysisCaseOp *cop;
   401	                        RzListIter *iter;
   402	                        RzList *unique_cases = rz_list_uniq(bb->switch_op->cases, casecmp, NULL);
   403	                        rz_list_foreach (unique_cases, iter, cop) {
   404	                                rz_cons_printf(" s 0x%08" PFMT64x, cop->addr);
   405	                        }
   406	                        rz_list_free(unique_cases);
   407	                }
   408	                rz_cons_newline();
   409	                break;
   410	        case RZ_OUTPUT_MODE_JSON: {
   411	                pj_o(pj);
   412	                if (bb->jump != UT64_MAX) {
   413	                        pj_kn(pj, "jump", bb->jump);
   414	                }
   415	                if (bb->fail != UT64_MAX) {
   416	                        pj_kn(pj, "fail", bb->fail);
   417	                }
   418	                if (bb->switch_op) {
   419	                        pj_k(pj, "switch_op");
   420	                        pj_o(pj);
   421	                        pj_kn(pj, "addr", bb->switch_op->addr);
   422	                        pj_kn(pj, "min_val", bb->switch_op->min_val);
   423	                        pj_kn(pj, "def_val", bb->switch_op->def_val);
   424	                        pj_kn(pj, "max_val", bb->switch_op->max_val);
   425	                        pj_k(pj, "cases");
   426	                        pj_a(pj);
   427	                        {
   428	                                RzListIter *case_op_iter;
   429	                                RzAnalysisCaseOp *case_op;
   430	                                rz_list_foreach (bb->switch_op->cases, case_op_iter, case_op) {
   431	                                        pj_o(pj);
   432	                                        pj_kn(pj, "addr", case_op->addr);
   433	                                        pj_kn(pj, "jump", case_op->jump);
   434	                                        pj_kn(pj, "value", case_op->value);
   435	                                        pj_end(pj);
   436	                                }
   437	                        }
   438	                        pj_end(pj);
   439	                        pj_end(pj);
   440	                }
   441	                pj_kn(pj, "opaddr", opaddr);
   442	                pj_kn(pj, "addr", bb->addr);
   443	                pj_ki(pj, "size", bb->size);
   444	                pj_ki(pj, "inputs", inputs);
   445	                pj_ki(pj, "outputs", outputs);
   446	                pj_ki(pj, "ninstr", bb->ninstr);
   447	                pj_kb(pj, "traced", bb->traced);
   448	                pj_end(pj);
   449	                break;
   450	        }
   451	        case RZ_OUTPUT_MODE_TABLE:
   452	                rz_table_add_rowf(t, "xdxx", bb->addr, bb->size, bb->jump, bb->fail);
   453	                break;
   454	        case RZ_OUTPUT_MODE_QUIET:
   455	                rz_cons_printf("0x%08" PFMT64x "\n", bb->addr);
   456	                break;
   457	        case RZ_OUTPUT_MODE_LONG: {
   458	                if (bb->jump != UT64_MAX) {
   459	                        rz_cons_printf("jump: 0x%08" PFMT64x "\n", bb->jump);
   460	                }
   461	                if (bb->fail != UT64_MAX) {
   462	                        rz_cons_printf("fail: 0x%08" PFMT64x "\n", bb->fail);
   463	                }
   464	                rz_cons_printf("opaddr: 0x%08" PFMT64x "\n", opaddr);
   465	                rz_cons_printf("addr: 0x%08" PFMT64x "\nsize: %" PFMT64d "\ninputs: %d\noutputs: %d\nninstr: %d\ntraced: %s\n",
   466	                        bb->addr, bb->size, inputs, outputs, bb->ninstr, rz_str_bool(bb->traced));
   467	                break;
   468	        }
   469	        default:
   470	                rz_warn_if_reached();
   471	                break;
   472	        }
   473	}
   474	
   475	static int bb_cmp(const void *a, const void *b, void *user) {
   476	        const RzAnalysisBlock *ba = a;
   477	        const RzAnalysisBlock *bb = b;
   478	        return ba->addr - bb->addr;
   479	}
   480	
   481	RZ_IPI void rz_core_analysis_bbs_info_print(RzCore *core, RzAnalysisFunction *fcn, RzCmdStateOutput *state) {
   482	        rz_return_if_fail(core && fcn && state);
   483	        void **iter;
   484	        RzAnalysisBlock *bb;
   485	        rz_cmd_state_output_array_start(state);
   486	        rz_cmd_state_output_set_columnsf(state, "xdxx", "addr", "size", "jump", "fail");
   487	
   488	        rz_pvector_sort(fcn->bbs, bb_cmp, NULL);
   489	        rz_pvector_foreach (fcn->bbs, iter) {
   490	                bb = (RzAnalysisBlock *)*iter;
   491	                bb_info_print(core, fcn, bb, bb->addr, state->mode, state->d.pj, state->d.t);
   492	        }
   493	
   494	        rz_cmd_state_output_array_end(state);
   495	}
   496

   498	        rz_return_if_fail(core && bb && state);
   499	        rz_cmd_state_output_set_columnsf(state, "xdxx", "addr", "size", "jump", "fail");
   500	        RzAnalysisFunction *fcn = rz_list_first_val(bb->fcns);
   501	        bb_info_print(core, fcn, bb, addr, state->mode, state->d.pj, state->d.t);
   502	}

   504	/*this only autoname those function that start with fcn.* or sym.func.* */
   505	RZ_API void rz_core_analysis_autoname_all_fcns(RzCore *core) {
   506	        RzListIter *it;
   507	        RzAnalysisFunction *fcn;
   508	
   509	        rz_list_foreach (core->analysis->fcns, it, fcn) {
   510	                if (!strncmp(fcn->name, "fcn.", 4) || !strncmp(fcn->name, "sym.func.", 9)) {
   511	                        RzFlagItem *item = rz_flag_get(core->flags, fcn->name);
   512	                        if (item) {
   513	                                char *name = rz_core_analysis_function_autoname(core, fcn);
   514	                                if (name) {
   515	                                        rz_flag_rename(core->flags, item, name);
   516	                                        free(fcn->name);
   517	                                        fcn->name = name;
   518	                                }
   519	                        } else {
   520	                                // there should always be a flag for a function
   521	                                rz_warn_if_reached();
   522	                        }
   523	                }
   524	        }
   525	}
   526	
   527	static bool blacklisted_word(const char *name) {
   528	        const char *list[] = {
   529	                "__stack_chk_guard",
   530	                "__stderrp",
   531	                "__stdinp",
   532	                "__stdoutp",
   533	                "_DefaultRuneLocale"
   534	        };
   535	        for (int i = 0; i < RZ_ARRAY_SIZE(list); i++) {
   536	                if (strstr(name, list[i])) {
   537	                        return true;
   538	                }
   539	        }
   540	        return false;
   541	}
   542

  4657	        int arch_align = rz_analysis_archinfo(core->analysis, RZ_ANALYSIS_ARCHINFO_TEXT_ALIGN);
  4658	        bool vinfun = rz_config_get_b(core->config, "analysis.vinfun");
  4659	        int searchAlign = rz_config_get_i(core->config, "search.align");
  4660	        int align = (arch_align < 1) ? searchAlign : arch_align;
  4661	        if (arch_align < 0 || (!aligns(from, align) || !aligns(to, align))) {
  4662	                // If archaling < 0, the arch has no alignment defined. Hence it is
  4663	                // ignored if isFine stays false.
  4664	                bool itsFine = false;
  4665	                if (archIsThumbable(core)) {
  4666	                        if ((from & 1) || (to & 1)) {
  4667	                                itsFine = true;
  4668	                        }
  4669	                }
  4670	                if (!itsFine) {
  4671	                        return;
  4672	                }

   453	        }

2026-05-28 08:08:14,906 - DEBUG - swea-lm - Response: ModelResponse(id='3b181c48643a49d7bcb40da51890d22a', created=1779955666, model='DeepSeek-V4-Pro', object='chat.completion', system_fingerprint=None, choices=[Choices(finish_reason='tool_calls', index=0, message=Message(content='Now let me look at the `cb_in_range_aav` more carefully. The issue is: when an ELF binary has `__stack_chk_guard` as an imported symbol, it\'s typically stored in the GOT (Global Offset Table). Now let me look at the `cb_in_range_aav` more carefully. The issue is: when an ELF binary has `__stack_chk_guard` as an imported symbol, it's typically stored in the GOT (Global Offset Table). The value at some address points to the GOT entry, and the GOT entry will be filled by the dynamic linker with the actual address of `__stack_chk_guard`. The binary analysis (`aav`) finds a value that points to the GOT entry for `__stack_chk_guard`, and creates a flag `aav.0x...` at the target address.

But the target address (e.g., `0x113b0`) might already have a flag from the binary's symbols (like `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`). The check at line 4684 should find that existing flag and skip creating the `aav` flag.

Wait, looking at the PR description again:
- `aav.aav.0x000113b0` should become `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` or just `__stack_chk_guard`
- The address `0x113b0` is referenced in the code, and it shows `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` in the comment

So the problem is that the flag at `0x113b0` is named `aav.aav.0x000113b0` instead of `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`. But wait, the `aav` flag is set at `to` (the target), not `from`. Let me re-examine:

In `cb_in_range_aav`:
- `from` is the address where a pointer value is found
- `to` is the value of that pointer (the target address)
- A flag `aav.0x0000...` is created at `to` if no flag exists there

But in the PR output:
```
0x000106ec      ldr   r3, [aav.aav.0x000113b0]             ; [0x113b0:4]=0x21f0c obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4
```

So `aav.aav.0x000113b0` is at address `0x113b0`, and the comment says `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`. This means the flag at `0x113b0` should be `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` but it's showing as `aav.aav.0x000113b0`.

So it would create `aav.0x000113b0`. But the output shows `aav.aav.0x000113b0`. That's strange.

Maybe the issue is about the xref name. Let me look at how xrefs are displayed. The comment `[0x113b0:4]=0x21f0c obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` shows that `0x113b0` contains the value `0x21f0c`, and `0x21f0c` is where `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` is located. So the `aav` flag should be at `0x21f0c` (the target), not `0x113b0`.

```

0x000106f0      ldr   r3, [r3]
```

So for an imported object symbol (like `__stack_chk_guard`), the prefix would be `obj.imp` (since it's imported). But in the PR description, they mention `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`. Let me look at how the flag is displayed in the disassembly.

Now, the key issue is about `aav` creating its own flag at an address that already has a symbol flag. Let me look at `cb_in_range_aav` again:

```c
if (!rz_flag_get_at(core->flags, to, false)) {
    char *name = rz_str_newf("aav.0x%08" PFMT64x, to);
    rz_flag_set(core->flags, name, to, vsize);
    free(name);
}
```

The `rz_flag_get_at` with `closest=false` only returns a flag if there's one at exactly the given offset. But what if the binary symbol flag was set with `rz_flag_set` at a different offset than the `to` address? Or what if the flag was set but is filtered by spaces?

Wait, I need to look at `IS_FI_NOTIN_SPACE` macro. Let me check that:

  1318	                cols /= 23;

    66	static Elf_(Word) get_number_of_symbols_from_heuristic_aux(ELFOBJ *bin, ut64 symtab_offset, ut64 strtab_offset) {
    67	        if (symtab_offset > strtab_offset) {
    68	                return 0;
    69	        }
    70	
    71	        ut64 symtab_size = strtab_offset - symtab_offset;
    72	        return symtab_size / sizeof(Elf_(Sym));
    73	}
    74	
    75	static Elf_(Word) get_number_of_symbols_from_heuristic(ELFOBJ *bin) {
    76	        ut64 symtab_addr;
    77	        ut64 strtab_addr;
    78	        if (!Elf_(rz_bin_elf_get_dt_info)(bin, DT_SYMTAB, &symtab_addr) || !Elf_(rz_bin_elf_get_dt_info)(bin, DT_STRTAB, &strtab_addr)) {
    79	                return 0;
    80	        }
    81	
    82	        ut64 symtab_offset = Elf_(rz_bin_elf_v2p)(bin, symtab_addr);
    83	        ut64 strtab_offset = Elf_(rz_bin_elf_v2p)(bin, strtab_addr);
    84	        if (symtab_offset == UT64_MAX || strtab_offset == UT64_MAX) {
    85	                return 0;
    86	        }
    87	
    88	        return get_number_of_symbols_from_heuristic_aux(bin, symtab_offset, strtab_offset);
    89	}
    90	
    91	static Elf_(Word) get_number_of_symbols_from_section(ELFOBJ *bin) {
    92	        RzBinElfSection *section = Elf_(rz_bin_elf_get_section_with_name)(bin, ".dynsym");
    93	        if (!section) {
    94	                return 0;
    95	        }
    96	
    97	        return section->size / sizeof(Elf_(Sym));
    98	}

   100	static bool is_special_arm_symbol(ELFOBJ *bin, Elf_(Sym) * sym, const char *name) {
   101	        if (!name) {
   102	                return false;
   103	        }
   104	
   105	        if (name[0] != '$') {
   106	                return false;
   107	        }
   108	
   109	        if (name[1] == 'a' || name[1] == 't' || name[1] == 'd' || name[1] == 'x') {
   110	                return (name[2] == '\0' || name[2] == '.') &&
   111	                        ELF_ST_TYPE(sym->st_info) == STT_NOTYPE &&
   112	                        ELF_ST_BIND(sym->st_info) == STB_LOCAL &&
   113	                        ELF_ST_VISIBILITY(sym->st_info) == STV_DEFAULT;
   114	        }
   115	
   116	        return false;
   117	}
   118	
   119	static bool is_special_symbol(ELFOBJ *bin, Elf_(Sym) * sym, const char *name) {
   120	        switch (bin->ehdr.e_machine) {
   121	        case EM_ARM:
   122	        case EM_AARCH64:
   123	                return is_special_arm_symbol(bin, sym, name);
   124	        default:
   125	                return false;
   126	        }
   127	}
   128	
   129	static const char *symbol_type_to_str(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfSymbol *ret, Elf_(Sym) * sym) {
   130	        if (bin && ret && is_special_symbol(bin, sym, ret->name)) {
   131	                return RZ_BIN_TYPE_SPECIAL_SYM_STR;
   132	        }
   133	
   134	        for (size_t i = 0; i < RZ_ARRAY_SIZE(symbol_type_translation_table); i++) {
   135	                if (ELF_ST_TYPE(sym->st_info) == symbol_type_translation_table[i].type) {
   136	                        return symbol_type_translation_table[i].name;
   137	                }
   138	        }
   139	
   140	        return RZ_BIN_TYPE_UNKNOWN_STR;
   141	}
   142

   147	                }
   148	        }

Actually, let me re-examine the PR description more carefully:

```
0x000106ec      ldr   r3, [aav.aav.0x000113b0]             ; [0x113b0:4]=0x21f0c obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4
```

The flag at `0x113b0` is `aav.aav.0x000113b0`. The comment says `[0x113b0:4]=0x21f0c obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`.

So:
- Address `0x113b0` has flag `aav.aav.0x000113b0`
- The value at `0x113b0` is `0x21f0c`
- Address `0x21f0c` has flag `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`

This means:
1. The binary properly flagged `0x21f0c` as `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` (the symbol itself)
2. `0x113b0` is a GOT entry or similar that points to `0x21f0c`
3. The `aav` analysis found the pointer at some address (maybe at `0x113b0` itself, or somewhere else) and created a flag at `0x113b0`

Wait, `aav` creates flags at `to` (the target of the pointer). If the pointer value is `0x21f0c`, then `to = 0x21f0c`. But `0x21f0c` already has a flag `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`, so `aav` should not create a flag there.

But the flag `aav.aav.0x000113b0` is at `0x113b0`, which is the `from` address (where the pointer is stored). So this flag must have been created by something else.

Let me look at `cb_in_range_aav` again. It does:
1. `rz_analysis_xrefs_set(core->analysis, from, to, RZ_ANALYSIS_XREF_TYPE_NULL);` - sets an xref from `from` to `to`
2. `rz_meta_set(core->analysis, RZ_META_TYPE_DATA, from, vsize, NULL);` - marks `from` as data
3. If no flag at `to`, creates `aav.0x...` at `to`

So the `aav` flag should be at `to`, not `from`. But the disassembly shows `aav.aav.0x000113b0` at `0x113b0` (which would be `from`). 

Unless... the flag at `0x113b0` was created by a different `aav` run where `0x113b0` was the `to` address (i.e., some other pointer pointed to `0x113b0`). But the name is `aav.aav.0x000113b0` with double `aav.` - that's suspicious.

So `rz_core_flag_get_by_spaces` searches flags in specific spaces. The `aav` flag might be in a different space (like `RZ_FLAGS_FS_SYMBOLS` or a default space) versus the binary symbol which would be in `RZ_FLAGS_FS_IMPORTS` or `RZ_FLAGS_FS_SYMBOLS`.

Now let me check where the `aav` flags are created. In `cb_in_range_aav`:
```c
if (!rz_flag_get_at(core->flags, to, false)) {
    char *name = rz_str_newf("aav.0x%08" PFMT64x, to);
    rz_flag_set(core->flags, name, to, vsize);
    free(name);
}
```

`rz_flag_set` sets the flag in the current flag space. `rz_flag_get_at` also checks only the current flag space (since it uses `IS_FI_NOTIN_SPACE`). So if the binary symbol flag is in a different space than the current one, `rz_flag_get_at` won't find it, and a new `aav` flag will be created.

But wait, that would explain why an `aav` flag is created at an address that already has a symbol flag. However, the flag name in the PR is `aav.aav.0x000113b0` (double `aav.`), which suggests something else is going on.

Let me check if there's another place that creates `aav` flags. Let me also check if the flag at `0x113b0` could have been created by the xref mechanism (since there's an xref set from `from` to `to`).

Actually, let me look at this from a different angle. Let me look at the specific ARM ELF handling for `__stack_chk_guard`. In ARM, `__stack_chk_guard` is typically a symbol in the `.got` section or similar. The issue might be related to how the GOT entries are handled in ARM.

But since I can't use git, let me focus on the code. The key issue is: when `aav` runs, it creates flags named `aav.0x...` at addresses that are targets of pointers. But sometimes these addresses already have proper symbol names (like `__stack_chk_guard`). The check `!rz_flag_get_at(core->flags, to, false)` should prevent this, but it might fail if the symbol flag is in a different flag space.

But the PR output shows `aav.aav.0x000113b0` at address `0x113b0`. And the comment says `[0x113b0:4]=0x21f0c obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`. So `0x113b0` is the GOT entry for `__stack_chk_guard`. The flag at `0x113b0` should be something like `reloc.__stack_chk_guard` or `obj.imp.__stack_chk_guard` but instead it's `aav.aav.0x000113b0`.

So the issue is that the GOT entry at `0x113b0` doesn't have a proper flag from the binary symbols, and `aav` is creating one. But wait, `aav` creates flags at `to` (the target address), not `from`. So `0x113b0` must be a `to` for some other pointer.

  1109	static void reloc_set_flag(RzCore *core, RzBinReloc *reloc, const char *prefix, ut64 flag_addr) {
  1110	        bool demangle = rz_config_get_b(core->config, "bin.demangle");
  1111	        char *reloc_name = construct_reloc_name(reloc, NULL, demangle);
  1112	        if (RZ_STR_ISEMPTY(reloc_name)) {
  1113	                free(reloc_name);
  1114	                return;
  1115	        }
  1116	        char *flag_name = NULL;
  1117	        if (core->bin->prefix) {
  1118	                flag_name = rz_str_newf("%s.%s.%s", core->bin->prefix, prefix, reloc_name);
  1119	        } else {
  1120	                flag_name = rz_str_newf("%s.%s", prefix, reloc_name);
  1121	        }
  1122	        rz_name_filter(flag_name, 0, true);
  1123	        RzFlagItem *existing = rz_flag_get(core->flags, flag_name);
  1124	        if (existing && existing->offset == flag_addr) {
  1125	                // Mostly important for target flags.
  1126	                // We don't want hundreds of reloc.target.<fcnname>.<xyz> flags at the same location
  1127	                free(reloc_name);
  1128	                free(flag_name);
  1129	                return;
  1130	        }
  1131	        RzFlagItem *fi = rz_flag_set_next(core->flags, flag_name, flag_addr, bin_reloc_size(reloc));
  1132	        if (fi) {
  1133	                rz_flag_item_set_realname(fi, reloc_name);
  1134	        }
  1135	
  1136	        free(reloc_name);
  1137	        free(flag_name);
  1138	}

  1078	static char *construct_reloc_name(RZ_NONNULL RzBinReloc *reloc, RZ_NULLABLE const char *name, bool demangle) {
  1079	        RzStrBuf *buf = rz_strbuf_new("");
  1080	
  1081	        // (optional) libname_
  1082	        if (reloc->import && reloc->import->libname) {
  1083	                rz_strbuf_appendf(buf, "%s_", reloc->import->libname);
  1084	        } else if (reloc->symbol && reloc->symbol->libname) {
  1085	                rz_strbuf_appendf(buf, "%s_", reloc->symbol->libname);
  1086	        }
  1087	
  1088	        // actual name
  1089	        if (name) {
  1090	                rz_strbuf_append(buf, name);
  1091	        } else if (demangle && reloc->import && RZ_STR_ISNOTEMPTY(reloc->import->dname)) {
  1092	                rz_strbuf_append(buf, reloc->import->dname);
  1093	        } else if (reloc->import && RZ_STR_ISNOTEMPTY(reloc->import->name)) {
  1094	                rz_strbuf_append(buf, reloc->import->name);
  1095	        } else if (demangle && reloc->symbol && RZ_STR_ISNOTEMPTY(reloc->symbol->dname)) {
  1096	                rz_strbuf_appendf(buf, "%s", reloc->symbol->dname);
  1097	        } else if (reloc->symbol && RZ_STR_ISNOTEMPTY(reloc->symbol->name)) {
  1098	                rz_strbuf_appendf(buf, "%s", reloc->symbol->name);
  1099	        } else if (reloc->is_ifunc) {
  1100	                // addend is the function pointer for the resolving ifunc
  1101	                rz_strbuf_appendf(buf, "ifunc_%" PFMT64x, reloc->addend);
  1102	        } else {
  1103	                rz_strbuf_set(buf, "");
  1104	        }
  1105	
  1106	        return rz_strbuf_drain(buf);
  1107	}
  1108

Only one place. So the double `aav.aav.` must come from something else. Let me check if there's a flag rename or if the disassembler adds a prefix.

Actually, wait. Let me look at the disassembly output again:

```
0x000106ec      ldr   r3, [aav.aav.0x000113b0]             ; [0x113b0:4]=0x21f0c obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4
```

Maybe the flag at `0x113b0` is actually named `aav.0x000113b0` (without the double `aav.`). And the display shows `aav.aav.0x000113b0` because there's an xref from another `aav` flag to this one? No, that doesn't make sense.

Let me check if the flag name is being displayed with its space prefix. Like if the space is "aav" and the flag is "aav.0x000113b0", it might show as "aav.aav.0x000113b0"? No, that's not how spaces work.

Actually, I wonder if the issue is about the flag name being `aav.aav.0x000113b0` because the `aav` analysis was run twice? Let me trace through the code:

1. First `aav` run: finds a pointer at some address `X` pointing to `0x113b0`. Creates flag `aav.0x000113b0` at `0x113b0`.
2. Second `aav` run: finds another pointer at some address `Y` also pointing to `0x113b0`. Checks `rz_flag_get_at(core->flags, 0x113b0, false)` - this should find the existing `aav.0x000113b0` flag and skip.

But if `rz_flag_get_at` doesn't find it (e.g., because of space mismatch), it would try to create `aav.0x000113b0` again. But `rz_flag_set` at line 686 checks if a flag with that name already exists:
```c
RzFlagItem *item = rz_flag_get(f, itemname);
```
If it does, it returns the existing one. So even if `rz_flag_get_at` fails, `rz_flag_set` would find the existing flag by name and not create a duplicate.

So the double `aav.aav.` can't come from running `aav` twice.

Let me check if there's another code path that creates flags with `aav.` prefix. Let me search more broadly:

    10	struct print_flag_t {
    11	        RzFlag *f;
    12	        PJ *pj;
    13	        RzTable *tbl;
    14	        bool in_range;
    15	        ut64 range_from;
    16	        ut64 range_to;
    17	        RzSpace *fs;
    18	        bool real;
    19	};

    21	static bool print_flag_name(RzFlagItem *flag, void *user) {
    22	        struct print_flag_t *u = (struct print_flag_t *)user;
    23	        if (u->in_range && (flag->offset < u->range_from || flag->offset >= u->range_to)) {
    24	                return true;
    25	        }
    26	        rz_cons_printf("%s\n", flag->name);
    27	        return true;
    28	}
    29	
    30	static bool print_flag_json(RzFlagItem *flag, void *user) {
    31	        struct print_flag_t *u = (struct print_flag_t *)user;
    32	        if (u->in_range && (flag->offset < u->range_from || flag->offset >= u->range_to)) {
    33	                return true;
    34	        }
    35	        const char *realname = RZ_STR_ISEMPTY(flag->realname) ? flag->name : flag->realname;
    36	        pj_o(u->pj);
    37	        pj_ks(u->pj, "name", flag->name);
    38	        pj_ks(u->pj, "realname", realname);
    39	        pj_ki(u->pj, "size", flag->size);
    40	        if (flag->alias) {
    41	                pj_ks(u->pj, "alias", flag->alias);
    42	        } else {
    43	                pj_kn(u->pj, "offset", flag->offset);
    44	        }
    45	        if (flag->comment) {
    46	                pj_ks(u->pj, "comment", flag->comment);
    47	        }
    48	        pj_end(u->pj);
    49	        return true;
    50	}
    51	
    52	static bool print_flag_orig_name(RzFlagItem *flag, void *user) {
    53	        struct print_flag_t *u = (struct print_flag_t *)user;
    54	        if (u->in_range && (flag->offset < u->range_from || flag->offset >= u->range_to)) {
    55	                return true;
    56	        }
    57	        if (flag->alias) {
    58	                const char *n = u->real ? flag->realname : flag->name;
    59	                rz_cons_printf("%s %" PFMT64d " %s\n", flag->alias, flag->size, n);
    60	        } else {
    61	                const char *n = u->real ? flag->realname : (u->f->realnames ? flag->realname : flag->name);
    62	                rz_cons_printf("0x%08" PFMT64x " %" PFMT64d " %s\n", flag->offset, flag->size, n);
    63	        }
    64	        return true;
    65	}
    66	
    67	static bool print_flag_table(RzFlagItem *flag, void *user) {
    68	        struct print_flag_t *u = (struct print_flag_t *)user;
    69	        if (u->in_range && (flag->offset < u->range_from || flag->offset >= u->range_to)) {
    70	                return true;
    71	        }
    72	        if (!RZ_STR_ISEMPTY(flag->name)) {
    73	                const char *spaceName = (flag->space && flag->space->name) ? flag->space->name : "";
    74	                const char *realname = RZ_STR_ISEMPTY(flag->realname) ? flag->name : flag->realname;
    75	                rz_table_add_rowf(u->tbl, "Xdsss", flag->offset, flag->size, spaceName, flag->name, realname);
    76	        }
    77	        return true;
    78	}
    79	
    80	static void flag_print(RzFlag *f, RzCmdStateOutput *state, ut64 range_from, ut64 range_to, bool in_range) {
    81	        rz_return_if_fail(f);
    82	        struct print_flag_t u = {
    83	                .f = f,
    84	                .in_range = in_range,
    85	                .range_from = range_from,
    86	                .range_to = range_to,
    87	                .real = false
    88	        };
    89	
    90	        switch (state->mode) {
    91	        case RZ_OUTPUT_MODE_QUIET:
    92	                rz_flag_foreach_space(f, rz_flag_space_cur(f), print_flag_name, &u);
    93	                break;
    94	        case RZ_OUTPUT_MODE_STANDARD:
    95	                rz_flag_foreach_space(f, rz_flag_space_cur(f), print_flag_orig_name, &u);
    96	                break;
    97	        case RZ_OUTPUT_MODE_JSON:
    98	                u.pj = state->d.pj;
    99	                pj_a(state->d.pj);
   100	                rz_flag_foreach_space(f, rz_flag_space_cur(f), print_flag_json, &u);
   101	                pj_end(state->d.pj);
   102	                break;
   103	        case RZ_OUTPUT_MODE_TABLE:
   104	                u.tbl = state->d.t;
   105	                rz_cmd_state_output_set_columnsf(state, "Xdsss", "addr", "size", "space", "name", "realname");
   106	                rz_flag_foreach_space(f, rz_flag_space_cur(f), print_flag_table, &u);
   107	                break;
   108	        default:
   109	                rz_warn_if_reached();
   110	                break;
   111	        }
   112	}
   113	
   114	RZ_IPI void rz_core_flag_print(RzFlag *f, RzCmdStateOutput *state) {
   115	        flag_print(f, state, UT64_MAX, UT64_MAX, false);
   116	}

   119	        struct print_flag_t u = {

   121	                .in_range = false,
   122	                .range_from = UT64_MAX,

I'm going off on a tangent. Let me focus on the core issue again.

The PR says:
- `aav.aav.0x000113b0` should become `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` or just `__stack_chk_guard`

The flag at `0x113b0` is `aav.aav.0x000113b0`. This is the GOT entry for `__stack_chk_guard`. The expected behavior is that this should be named after the symbol it points to.

But wait, looking at the comment: `[0x113b0:4]=0x21f0c obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` - this comment shows the value at `0x113b0` is `0x21f0c`, and `0x21f0c` is where `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` is.

So the proper behavior would be that `0x113b0` gets a flag like `reloc.__stack_chk_guard` (since it's a GOT entry that points to `__stack_chk_guard`). But the `aav` analysis creates `aav.0x000113b0` instead.

Now, why does `aav` create a flag at `0x113b0`? Because some pointer somewhere points to `0x113b0`. But `0x113b0` is the GOT entry itself, which contains a pointer to `0x21f0c`. So the `aav` analysis should find:
1. At the GOT entry `0x113b0`, the value `0x21f0c` points to `__stack_chk_guard`. So it should create a flag at `0x21f0c` (but that already has `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`).
2. Some code references `0x113b0` (like `ldr r3, [0x113b0]`), which is a pointer to the GOT entry.

But the flag at `0x113b0` is `aav.aav.0x000113b0`. How did it get that? There must be some other pointer that points to `0x113b0` itself (like the code at `0x106ec` which loads from `0x113b0`). The `aav` analysis might find the instruction encoding (which contains the offset `0x113b0`) and treat it as a pointer to `0x113b0`.

Actually, that's likely what happens: the `aav` analysis scans through the binary looking for values that look like pointers. When it finds the instruction `ldr r3, [0x113b0]`, the immediate value `0x113b0` is encoded in the instruction, and `aav` reads it and thinks it's a pointer to `0x113b0`. So it creates a flag `aav.0x000113b0` at `0x113b0`.

But `0x113b0` should already have a relocation flag (like `reloc.__stack_chk_guard`). The issue is that `aav` doesn't check for existing flags properly (maybe due to flag spaces), and creates a duplicate.

Wait, but the flag name is `aav.aav.0x000113b0` with double `aav.`. Let me check if perhaps the `aav` analysis is called from within a flag space that already has `aav.` prefix?

This is a flags database file. It shows the format. Now let me think about the problem differently.

Maybe the issue is not about `aav` at all. Let me re-read the PR description:

> `aav.aav.0x000113b0` should become `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` or just `__stack_chk_guard`

The flag name is `aav.aav.0x000113b0`. The expected name is `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` or `__stack_chk_guard`.

Now, `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` is the name of the symbol at `0x21f0c` (the actual `__stack_chk_guard` in libc). The GOT entry at `0x113b0` points to it. 

So the question is: why is the GOT entry at `0x113b0` named `aav.aav.0x000113b0`? It should have a relocation flag like `reloc.__stack_chk_guard` (or `reloc.GLIBC_2.4___stack_chk_guard`).

Maybe the relocation flags are not being created properly for ARM? Let me look at the ELF relocation handling for ARM.

    19	bool Elf_(rz_bin_elf_is_arm_binary_supporting_thumb)(RZ_NONNULL ELFOBJ *bin) {
    20	        rz_return_val_if_fail(bin, false);
    21	        return bin->ehdr.e_machine == EM_ARM;

    24	bool Elf_(rz_bin_elf_is_thumb_addr)(ut64 addr) {
    25	        return addr != UT64_MAX && addr & 1;
    26	}
    27	
    28	void Elf_(rz_bin_elf_fix_arm_thumb_addr)(ut64 *addr) {
    29	        rz_return_if_fail(Elf_(rz_bin_elf_is_thumb_addr(*addr)));
    30	        *addr -= 1;
    31	}
    32	
    33	void Elf_(rz_bin_elf_fix_arm_thumb_object)(RZ_NONNULL ut64 *paddr, RZ_NONNULL ut64 *vaddr, RZ_NONNULL int *bits) {

    37

    39	                Elf_(rz_bin_elf_fix_arm_thumb_addr)(paddr);

    46	        }

    10	struct relocs_segment {
    11	        ut64 offset;
    12	        ut64 size;
    13	        ut64 entry_size;
    14	        ut64 mode;
    15	};
    16	
    17	static struct relocs_segment relocs_segment_init(ut64 offset, ut64 size, ut64 entry_size, ut64 mode) {
    18	        return (struct relocs_segment){
    19	                .offset = offset,
    20	                .size = size,
    21	                .entry_size = entry_size,
    22	                .mode = mode,
    23	        };
    24	}
    25	
    26	static void fix_rva_and_offset_relocable_file(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *reloc, RzBinElfSection *section) {
    27	        RzBinElfSection *sub_section = Elf_(rz_bin_elf_get_section)(bin, section->info);
    28	
    29	        if (!sub_section) {
    30	                reloc->paddr = UT64_MAX;
    31	                reloc->vaddr = reloc->offset;
    32	        } else {
    33	                reloc->paddr = sub_section->offset + reloc->offset;
    34	                reloc->vaddr = Elf_(rz_bin_elf_p2v)(bin, reloc->paddr);
    35	                reloc->section = section->info;
    36	        }
    37	}
    38	
    39	static void fix_rva_and_offset_exec_file(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *reloc) {
    40	        reloc->paddr = Elf_(rz_bin_elf_v2p)(bin, reloc->offset);
    41	
    42	        if (reloc->paddr == UT64_MAX) {
    43	                reloc->paddr = reloc->offset;
    44	        }
    45	
    46	        reloc->vaddr = reloc->offset;
    47	}
    48	
    49	static void fix_rva_and_offset(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *reloc, RzBinElfSection *section) {
    50	        if (section && Elf_(rz_bin_elf_is_relocatable)(bin)) {
    51	                fix_rva_and_offset_relocable_file(bin, reloc, section);
    52	        } else {
    53	                fix_rva_and_offset_exec_file(bin, reloc);
    54	        }
    55	}
    56	
    57	static ut64 get_size_rel_mode(ut64 mode) {
    58	        return mode == DT_REL ? sizeof(Elf_(Rel)) : sizeof(Elf_(Rela));
    59	}
    60	
    61	static bool read_reloc_entry_aux(ELFOBJ *bin, Elf_(Rela) * reloc, ut64 offset, ut64 mode) {
    62	        if (!Elf_(rz_bin_elf_read_addr)(bin, &offset, &reloc->r_offset) ||
    63	                !Elf_(rz_bin_elf_read_word_xword)(bin, &offset, &reloc->r_info)) {
    64	                return false;
    65	        }
    66	
    67	        if (mode == DT_REL) {
    68	                reloc->r_addend = 0;
    69	                return true;
    70	        }
    71	
    72	        return Elf_(rz_bin_elf_read_sword_sxword)(bin, &offset, &reloc->r_addend);
    73	}
    74	
    75	static bool read_reloc_entry(ELFOBJ *bin, Elf_(Rela) * reloc, ut64 offset, ut64 mode) {
    76	        if (!read_reloc_entry_aux(bin, reloc, offset, mode)) {
    77	                RZ_LOG_WARN("Failed to read reloc at 0x%" PFMT64x ".\n", offset);
    78	                return false;
    79	        }
    80	
    81	        return true;
    82	}
    83	
    84	/**
    85	 * In mipsel64 objects r_info isn't really a le64 value
    86	 * it has a le32 symbol index followed by 4 byte fields.
    87	 *
    88	 * this function reorders the information field accordingly.
    89	 */
    90	static void fix_elf_rel_mipsel64(ELFOBJ *bin, Elf_(Rela) * tmp) {
    91	        if (bin->ehdr.e_machine != EM_MIPS ||
    92	                bin->ehdr.e_ident[EI_CLASS] != ELFCLASS64 ||
    93	                bin->ehdr.e_ident[EI_DATA] == ELFDATA2MSB) {
    94	                // ignore if not MIPS64 little endian
    95	                return;
    96	        }
    97	
    98	        ut64 info = tmp->r_info;
    99	        info = (((info & 0xffffffffull) << 32) | ((info >> 56) & 0xffull) | ((info >> 40) & 0xff00ull) | ((info >> 24) & 0xff0000ull) | ((info >> 8) & 0xff000000ull));
   100	        tmp->r_info = info;

     8	RZ_OWN RzBinSymbol *Elf_(rz_bin_elf_convert_symbol)(RZ_NONNULL ELFOBJ *bin, RZ_NONNULL RzBinElfSymbol *elf_symbol) {
     9	        rz_return_val_if_fail(bin && elf_symbol, NULL);
    10	
    11	        RzBinSymbol *result = RZ_NEW0(RzBinSymbol);
    12	        if (!result) {
    13	                return NULL;
    14	        }
    15	
    16	        result->paddr = elf_symbol->paddr;
    17	        result->vaddr = elf_symbol->vaddr;
    18	        if (RZ_STR_ISNOTEMPTY(elf_symbol->name)) {
    19	                result->name = rz_str_dup(elf_symbol->name);
    20	        } else {
    21	                result->name = rz_str_newf("unknown_%u", elf_symbol->ordinal);
    22	        }
    23	        result->forwarder = "NONE";
    24	        result->bind = elf_symbol->bind;
    25	        result->type = elf_symbol->type;
    26	        result->size = elf_symbol->size;
    27	        result->ordinal = elf_symbol->ordinal;
    28	        result->bits = bin->bits;
    29	
    30	        if (Elf_(rz_bin_elf_is_arm_binary_supporting_thumb)(bin)) {
    31	                Elf_(rz_bin_elf_fix_arm_thumb_symbol)(result);
    32	        }
    33	
    34	        return result;
    35	}
    36	
    37	static RzBinSymbol *get_symbol(ELFOBJ *bin, ut32 ordinal) {
    38	        RzBinElfSymbol *symbol = Elf_(rz_bin_elf_get_symbol)(bin, ordinal);
    39	        if (!symbol) {
    40	                return NULL;
    41	        }
    42	
    43	        return Elf_(rz_bin_elf_convert_symbol)(bin, symbol);
    44	}
    45	
    46	RZ_OWN RzBinImport *Elf_(rz_bin_elf_convert_import)(RZ_NONNULL RzBinElfSymbol *elf_symbol) {
    47	        rz_return_val_if_fail(elf_symbol, NULL);
    48	
    49	        RzBinImport *result = RZ_NEW0(RzBinImport);
    50	        if (!result) {
    51	                return NULL;
    52	        }
    53	
    54	        result->name = rz_str_dup(elf_symbol->name);
    55	        result->bind = elf_symbol->bind;
    56	        result->type = elf_symbol->type;
    57	        result->ordinal = elf_symbol->ordinal;
    58	
    59	        return result;
    60	}
    61	
    62	static RzBinImport *get_import(RZ_NONNULL ELFOBJ *bin, ut32 ordinal) {
    63	        RzBinElfSymbol *symbol = Elf_(rz_bin_elf_get_import)(bin, ordinal);
    64	        if (!symbol) {
    65	                return NULL;
    66	        }
    67	
    68	        return Elf_(rz_bin_elf_convert_import)(symbol);
    69	}
    70	
    71	static RzBinReloc *reloc_convert_new(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *rel) {
    72	        RzBinReloc *reloc = RZ_NEW0(RzBinReloc);
    73	        if (!reloc) {
    74	                return NULL;
    75	        }
    76	
    77	        reloc->addend = rel->addend;
    78	        if (rel->sym) {
    79	                reloc->import = get_import(bin, rel->sym);
    80	                if (!reloc->import) {
    81	                        reloc->symbol = get_symbol(bin, rel->sym);
    82	                }
    83	        }
    84	        reloc->vaddr = rel->vaddr;
    85	        reloc->paddr = rel->paddr;
    86	        reloc->target_vaddr = rel->target_vaddr;
    87	
    88	        RzBinElfSection *section = rel->section > 0 ? Elf_(rz_bin_elf_get_section)(bin, rel->section) : NULL;
    89	        if (section) {
    90	                reloc->section_vaddr = section->rva;
    91	        }
    92	        return reloc;
    93	}
    94	
    95	static RzBinReloc *reloc_convert_set(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *rel, RzBinRelocType type, const char *print_name) {
    96	        RzBinReloc *r = reloc_convert_new(bin, rel);
    97	        if (!r) {
    98	                return NULL;
    99	        }
   100	
   101	        r->type = type;
   102	        r->print_name = print_name;
   103	        return r;
   104	}
   105	
   106	static RzBinReloc *reloc_convert_add(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *rel, RzBinRelocType type, st64 addend, const char *print_name, RzRelocBase reloc_base) {
   107	        RzBinReloc *r = reloc_convert_new(bin, rel);
   108	        if (!r) {
   109	                return NULL;
   110	        }
   111	
   112	        r->reloc_base = reloc_base;
   113	        r->addend += addend;
   114	        r->type = type;
   115	        r->print_name = print_name;
   116	        return r;
   117	}
   118	
   119	static RzBinReloc *reloc_convert_ifunc(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *rel, RzBinRelocType type, const char *print_name) {
   120	        RzBinReloc *r = reloc_convert_new(bin, rel);
   121	        if (!r) {
   122	                return NULL;
   123	        }
   124	
   125	        r->is_ifunc = true;
   126	        r->type = type;
   127	        r->print_name = print_name;
   128	        return r;
   129	}
   130	
   131	// Custom function for arm specific relocations
   132	static RzBinReloc *reloc_convert_add_t(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *rel, RzBinRelocType type, bool T, st64 addend, const char *print_name, RzRelocBase reloc_base) {
   133	        RzBinReloc *r = reloc_convert_new(bin, rel);
   134	        if (!r) {
   135	                return NULL;
   136	        }
   137	
   138	        r->reloc_base = reloc_base;
   139	        r->addend = (r->addend | T) + addend;
   140	        r->type = type;
   141	        r->print_name = print_name;
   142	        return r;
   143	}
   144	
   145	#define UNSUPP_RELOC(NAME, V) \
   146	        RZ_LOG_WARN(NAME ": Unsupported conversion of ELF reloc %u (0x%x)\n", (ut32)V, (ut32)V); \
   147	        return reloc_convert_new(bin, rel)
   148	
   149	#define UNSUPP(NAME) UNSUPP_RELOC(NAME, rel->type)
   150	
   151	#define UNHANDL(RELOC_NAME) \
   152	        RZ_LOG_WARN("Unhandled conversion of ELF reloc %d (" RELOC_NAME ")\n", rel->type); \
   153	        return reloc_convert_set(bin, rel, 0, RELOC_NAME)
   154	
   155	#define SET(BIT_SIZE, RELOC_NAME) \
   156	        return reloc_convert_set(bin, rel, RZ_BIN_RELOC_##BIT_SIZE, RELOC_NAME)
   157	
   158	#define ADD(BIT_SIZE, ADDEND, RELOC_NAME, RELOC_BASE) \
   159	        return reloc_convert_add(bin, rel, RZ_BIN_RELOC_##BIT_SIZE, ADDEND, RELOC_NAME, RELOC_BASE)
   160	
   161	#define IFCN(BIT_SIZE, RELOC_NAME) \
   162	        return reloc_convert_ifunc(bin, rel, RZ_BIN_RELOC_##BIT_SIZE, RELOC_NAME)
   163	
   164	/*
   165	Custom add for arm specific relocations
   166	We do A|T this would result the same as (S+A)|T
   167	*/
   168	#define ADD_T(BIT_SIZE, T, ADDEND, RELOC_NAME, RELOC_BASE) \
   169	        return reloc_convert_add_t(bin, rel, RZ_BIN_RELOC_##BIT_SIZE, T, ADDEND, RELOC_NAME, RELOC_BASE)
   170	
   171	static RzBinReloc *reloc_convert_intel_80386(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *rel, ut64 GOT) {
   172	        ut64 P = rel->vaddr;
   173	
   174	        switch (rel->type) {
   175	        case R_386_NONE:
   176	                return reloc_convert_set(bin, rel, 0, "R_386_NONE");
   177	        case R_386_32: ADD(32, 0, "R_386_32", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   178	        case R_386_PC32: ADD(32, -P, "R_386_PC32", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   179	        case R_386_GLOB_DAT: SET(32, "R_386_GLOB_DAT");
   180	        case R_386_JMP_SLOT: SET(32, "R_386_JMP_SLOT");
   181	        case R_386_RELATIVE: ADD(32, 0, "R_386_RELATIVE", RZ_RELOC_BASE_BASE);
   182	        case R_386_GOTOFF: ADD(32, -GOT, "R_386_GOTOFF", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   183	        case R_386_GOTPC: ADD(32, GOT - P, "R_386_GOTPC", RZ_RELOC_BASE_GOT);
   184	        case R_386_16: ADD(16, 0, "R_386_16", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   185	        case R_386_PC16: ADD(16, -P, "R_386_PC16", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   186	        case R_386_8: ADD(8, 0, "R_386_8", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   187	        case R_386_PC8: ADD(8, -P, "R_386_PC8", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   188	        case R_386_COPY: ADD(32, 0, "R_386_COPY", RZ_RELOC_BASE_UNKNOWN); // copy symbol at runtime
   189	        case R_386_IRELATIVE: IFCN(32, "R_386_IRELATIVE");
   190	        case R_386_32PLT: ADD(32, 0, "R_386_32PLT", RZ_RELOC_BASE_PLT_SYMBOL);
   191	        case R_386_TLS_TPOFF: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_TPOFF");
   192	        case R_386_TLS_IE: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_IE");
   193	        case R_386_TLS_GOTIE: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_GOTIE");
   194	        case R_386_TLS_LE: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_LE");
   195	        case R_386_TLS_GD: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_GD");
   196	        case R_386_TLS_LDM: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_LDM");
   197	        case R_386_TLS_GD_32: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_GD_32");
   198	        case R_386_TLS_GD_PUSH: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_GD_PUSH");
   199	        case R_386_TLS_GD_CALL: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_GD_CALL");
   200	        case R_386_TLS_GD_POP: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_GD_POP");
   201	        case R_386_TLS_LDM_32: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_LDM_32");
   202	        case R_386_TLS_LDM_PUSH: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_LDM_PUSH");
   203	        case R_386_TLS_LDM_CALL: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_LDM_CALL");
   204	        case R_386_TLS_LDM_POP: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_LDM_POP");
   205	        case R_386_TLS_LDO_32: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_LDO_32");

   211	        case R_386_SIZE32: UNHANDL("R_386_SIZE32");
   212	        case R_386_TLS_GOTDESC: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_GOTDESC");
   213	        case R_386_TLS_DESC_CALL: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_DESC_CALL");
   214	        case R_386_TLS_DESC: UNHANDL("R_386_TLS_DESC");

   222	static RzBinReloc *reloc_convert_amd64(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *rel, ut64 GOT) {
   223	        ut64 P = rel->vaddr;
   224	
   225	        switch (rel->type) {
   226	        case R_X86_64_NONE:
   227	                return reloc_convert_set(bin, rel, 0, "R_X86_64_NONE");
   228	        case R_X86_64_64: ADD(64, 0, "R_X86_64_64", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   229	        case R_X86_64_PLT32: ADD(32, -P /* +L */, "R_X86_64_PLT32", RZ_RELOC_BASE_PLT_SYMBOL);
   230	        case R_X86_64_GOT32: ADD(32, GOT, "R_X86_64_GOT32", RZ_RELOC_BASE_GOT_SYMBOL);
   231	        case R_X86_64_PC32: ADD(32, -P, "R_X86_64_PC32", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   232	        case R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT: ADD(64, 0, "R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   233	        case R_X86_64_JUMP_SLOT: ADD(64, 0, "R_X86_64_JUMP_SLOT", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   234	        case R_X86_64_RELATIVE: ADD(64, 0, "R_X86_64_RELATIVE", RZ_RELOC_BASE_BASE);
   235	        case R_X86_64_32: ADD(32, 0, "R_X86_64_32", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   236	        case R_X86_64_32S: ADD(32, 0, "R_X86_64_32S", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   237	        case R_X86_64_16: ADD(16, 0, "R_X86_64_16", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   238	        case R_X86_64_PC16: ADD(16, -P, "R_X86_64_PC16", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);

   282	
   283	static RzBinReloc *reloc_convert_arm(ELFOBJ *bin, RzBinElfReloc *rel, ut64 GOT) {
   284	        ut64 B = bin->baddr;
   285	        ut64 P = rel->vaddr;
   286	        ut64 Pa = P & 0xfffffffc;
   287	        bool T = 0;
   288	        RzBinElfSymbol *symbol = Elf_(rz_bin_elf_get_symbol)(bin, rel->sym);
   289	        if (symbol && RZ_STR_EQ(symbol->type, RZ_BIN_TYPE_FUNC_STR)) {
   290	                // Check for LSB to know if symbol addresses thumb instruction
   291	                T = (symbol->vaddr & 1) ? 1 : 0;
   292	        }
   293	
   294	        switch (rel->type) {
   295	        case R_ARM_NONE:
   296	                return reloc_convert_set(bin, rel, 0, "R_ARM_NONE");
   297	        case R_ARM_ABS32: ADD_T(32, T, 0, "R_ARM_ABS32", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   298	        case R_ARM_REL32: ADD_T(32, T, -P, "R_ARM_REL32", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   299	        case R_ARM_ABS16: ADD(16, 0, "R_ARM_ABS16", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   300	        case R_ARM_ABS8: ADD(8, 0, "R_ARM_ABS8", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   301	        case R_ARM_SBREL32: ADD_T(32, T, B, "R_ARM_SBREL32", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   302	        case R_ARM_GLOB_DAT: ADD_T(32, T, 0, "R_ARM_GLOB_DAT", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   303	        case R_ARM_JUMP_SLOT: ADD_T(32, T, 0, "R_ARM_JUMP_SLOT", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   304	        case R_ARM_COPY: ADD(32, 0, "R_ARM_COPY", RZ_RELOC_BASE_UNKNOWN); // copy symbol at runtime
   305	        case R_ARM_RELATIVE: ADD(32, 0, "R_ARM_RELATIVE", RZ_RELOC_BASE_BASE);
   306	        case R_ARM_GOTOFF32: ADD_T(32, T, -GOT, "R_ARM_GOTOFF32", RZ_RELOC_BASE_SYMBOL);
   307	        case R_ARM_BASE_PREL: ADD(32, -P, "R_ARM_GOTPC", RZ_RELOC_BASE_BASE);

Now I see the ARM relocation handling. The `reloc_convert_arm` function handles various ARM relocation types. The issue might be related to how `__stack_chk_guard` is handled specifically.

But let me step back and think about the actual problem differently. The PR says `aav.aav.0x000113b0` should become `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` or just `__stack_chk_guard`.

Looking at the disassembly:
- At `0x106ec`, instruction `ldr r3, [0x113b0]` loads from `0x113b0`
- `0x113b0` contains `0x21f0c`, which is the address of `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`
- The flag at `0x113b0` is shown as `aav.aav.0x000113b0`

The expected behavior is that the flag at `0x113b0` should be something meaningful like `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` or `__stack_chk_guard`.

But wait, `__stack_chk_guard` is at `0x21f0c`, not `0x113b0`. The PR says `aav.aav.0x000113b0` should become `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` or just `__stack_chk_guard`. This is confusing - how can `0x113b0` and `0x21f0c` have the same name?

Actually, maybe the PR is saying that the flag at `0x113b0` should be named after what it points to? Or maybe the expected behavior is that the disassembler should resolve through the GOT and show the final symbol?

Let me look at the disassembly comment: `[0x113b0:4]=0x21f0c obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`. This shows that the value at `0x113b0` is `0x21f0c`, which is `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`. So the disassembler already resolves the pointer and shows the target symbol in the comment. The issue is that the operand itself shows `[aav.aav.0x000113b0]` instead of something more readable.

So the fix should be: the flag at `0x113b0` should not be `aav.aav.0x000113b0`. It should be something like `reloc.__stack_chk_guard` (since it's a GOT entry for that symbol). The relocation processing should create that flag.

But the PR description says this is an `aav` issue. The `aav` analysis creates the flag `aav.0x000113b0` at `0x113b0` because:
1. Some value somewhere points to `0x113b0`
2. `aav` finds this and creates a flag

But `0x113b0` should already have a relocation flag (from the ELF relocations). If it doesn't, that's the bug.

Let me check how relocations are applied and flagged. Specifically, look at `set_bin_relocs` and `reloc_set_flag`:

From `cbin.c`:
- Line 1200: `reloc_set_flag(r, reloc, "reloc", addr);` - creates `reloc.<name>` at the reloc's address
- Line 1202: `reloc_set_flag(r, reloc, "reloc.target", reloc->target_vaddr);` - creates `reloc.target.<name>` at the target address

So for `__stack_chk_guard`:
- There should be a relocation entry at `0x113b0` (the GOT entry)
- `reloc_set_flag` would create `reloc.__stack_chk_guard` at `0x113b0`
- And `reloc.target.__stack_chk_guard` at `0x21f0c` (the actual symbol)

But the PR shows `aav.aav.0x000113b0` at `0x113b0`, meaning the relocation flag wasn't created or wasn't found.

Wait, I notice the double `aav.` again. Let me check if there's a flag space named "aav" or something that would cause this.

Actually, let me look at line 1222: `rz_flag_space_push(core->flags, RZ_FLAGS_FS_RELOCS);`

So relocs are created in the "relocs" space. Later, when `aav` runs, it's in a different space (probably the default "*" space). The `aav` function `cb_in_range_aav` checks `!rz_flag_get_at(core->flags, to, false)` to see if there's already a flag at the target address. But `rz_flag_get_at` only finds flags in the current space. So if the reloc flag is in "relocs" space and the current space is "*", it won't find it and will create an `aav` flag.

This is the bug! The fix should be to check for flags across all relevant spaces, or at least ensure the `aav` analysis checks the right spaces.

But wait, the flag name is `aav.aav.0x000113b0` with double `aav.`. That's still puzzling. Let me check if there's a flag space called "aav" that might be pushed before running `aav`

And the expected behavior: `aav.aav.0x000113b0` should become `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` or just `__stack_chk_guard`

So the flag at `0x113b0` is currently `aav.aav.0x000113b0` and should be `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` or `__stack_chk_guard`.

But looking at the disassembly:
- `0x113b0` contains `0x21f0c` which is the address of `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`
- So `0x113b0` is the GOT entry for `__stack_chk_guard`
- The expected name is `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4` (same as the target symbol)

This means the GOT entry should be named after the symbol it points to. This could be achieved by:
1. The relocation processing creating a flag `reloc.__stack_chk_guard` at `0x113b0` (which it should)
2. Or the `aav` analysis creating a flag with the target's name instead of `aav.0x...`

But the PR says the expected name is `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`, which is the target symbol's flag name. So maybe the fix is that when `aav` creates a flag at `to`, if `to` already has a flag in some space, it should use that flag's name (or not create a new flag at all). Or when `to` points to a known symbol, the flag at `from` should be named after that symbol.

Wait, I'm confusing `from` and `to`. Let me re-examine `cb_in_range_aav`:

```c
rz_analysis_xrefs_set(core->analysis, from, to, RZ_ANALYSIS_XREF_TYPE_NULL);
rz_meta_set(core->analysis, RZ_META_TYPE_DATA, from, vsize, NULL);
if (!rz_flag_get_at(core->flags, to, false)) {
    char *name = rz_str_newf("aav.0x%08" PFMT64x, to);
    rz_flag_set(core->flags, name, to, vsize);
    free(name);
}
```

- `from` = the address where a pointer value was found (e.g., inside an instruction like `ldr r3, [0x113b0]`)
- `to` = the value of the pointer (e.g., `0x113b0`)

So if the instruction at `0x106ec` is `ldr r3, [0x113b0]`, the value `0x113b0` is embedded in the instruction. The `aav` analysis finds this value and treats it as a pointer. So `from = 0x106ec` (or wherever the value was read from), `to = 0x113b0`.

Then it creates a flag `aav.0x000113b0` at `0x113b0`. That's why the flag at `0x113b0` is `aav.0x...`.

Now, `0x113b0` is the GOT entry for `__stack_chk_guard`. The relocation processing should have already created a flag `reloc.__stack_chk_guard` at `0x113b0` (in the "relocs" space). But `cb_in_range_aav` checks `rz_flag_get_at(core->flags, to, false)` which only looks in the current space. If the current space is not "relocs", it won't find the existing flag and will create a duplicate `aav` flag.

So the fix is to use a cross-space flag lookup in `cb_in_range_aav`. Let me change `rz_flag_get_at` to `rz_core_flag_get_by_spaces` or similar.

But wait, there's another issue. The flag name `aav.aav.0x000113b0` has double `aav.`. Let me check if this could be caused by the xref mechanism. When `rz_analysis_xrefs_set` is called, it creates an xref from `from` to `to`. Later, when the disassembler displays the operand at `from`, it might show the xref information. But the operand itself shows `[aav.aav.0x000113b0]`, which means the flag at `0x113b0` is being used as the operand string.

Let me check if there's a mechanism that renames flags based on xrefs. Actually, I think the double `aav.` might be because the flag is in a space called "aav"? No, there's no such space.

Let me look at the filter.c code again. At line 283-288:

```c
flag = p->flag_get(f, off);
if ((!flag || arm) && p->subrel_addr) {
    remove_brackets = lea || (arm && p->subrel_addr);
    flag2 = p->flag_get(f, p->subrel_addr);
    if (!flag || arm) {
        flag = flag2;
    }
}
```

For ARM, if there's a `subrel_addr`, it prefers the flag at `subrel_addr`. This might cause the flag name to be something unexpected.

But I'm going too deep into the display logic. Let me focus on the core fix: `cb_in_range_aav` should check for existing flags across all relevant spaces before creating an `aav` flag.

Let me also check if maybe the issue is that `__stack_chk_guard` is not being treated as an import/symbol properly in ARM ELF. Let me check the `is_special_arm_symbol` function:

```c
static bool is_special_arm_symbol(ELFOBJ *bin, Elf_(Sym) * sym, const char *name) {
    if (!name) {
        return false;
    }
    if (name[0] != '$') {
        return false;
    }
    if (name[1] == 'a' || name[1] == 't' || name[1] == 'd' || name[1] == 'x') {
        return (name[2] == '\0' || name[2] == '.') &&
            ELF_ST_TYPE(sym->st_info) == STT_NOTYPE &&
            ELF_ST_BIND(sym->st_info) == STB_LOCAL &&
            ELF_ST_VISIBILITY(sym->st_info) == STV_DEFAULT;
    }
    return false;
}
```

This only affects symbols starting with `$`. `__stack_chk_guard` doesn't start with `$`, so it's not affected.

Let me look at the `get_prefix_for_sym` function again:

```c
static const char *get_prefix_for_sym(const RzBinSymbol *sym) {
    if (!sym) {
        return "sym";
    }
    // workaround for ELF
    if (sym->type) {
        if (!strcmp(sym->type, RZ_BIN_TYPE_NOTYPE_STR)) {
            return sym->is_imported ? "loc.imp" : "loc";
        }
        if (!strcmp(sym->type, RZ_BIN_TYPE_OBJECT_STR)) {
            return sym->is_imported ? "obj.imp" : "obj";
        }
    }
    return sym->is_imported ? "sym.imp" : "sym";
}
```

So for an imported object symbol (like `__stack_chk_guard`), the prefix would be `obj.imp`. And with libname, it would become `obj.imp.GLIBC_2.4___stack_chk_guard` or similar.

But the PR shows `obj.__stack_chk_guard__GLIBC_2.4`. That's `obj.` prefix (not `obj.imp`), suggesting the symbol is not imported but is a regular object symbol. Wait, maybe `__stack_chk_guard` is a local symbol in the binary? Actually, `__stack_chk_guard` is typically an imported symbol from libc. But it could also be a local symbol if statically linked.

Hmm, I think I should stop guessing and look at the actual code change that would fix this. Let me check if there's a way to see what changes were made to fix this in the rizin repository. Since I can't use git, let me look at the code history through the files themselves.

Actually, let me look at the `cb_in_range_aav` function more carefully. The issue might be that `rz_flag_get_at` returns NULL because the flag is in a different space, but also the `aav` flag gets a double prefix because of how the flag is named or how it's displayed.

Let me just make the fix and test it. The most obvious fix is to change the flag existence check in `cb_in_range_aav` to use a cross-space lookup:

```c
// Old:
if (!rz_flag_get_at(core->flags, to, false)) {

// New:
if (!rz_flag_get_by_spaces(core->flags, to, 
    RZ_FLAGS_FS_FUNCTIONS,
    RZ_FLAGS_FS_SYMBOLS,
    RZ_FLAGS_FS_IMPORTS,
    RZ_FLAGS_FS_RELOCS,
    RZ_FLAGS_FS_STRINGS,

    RZ_FLAGS_FS_SEGMENTS,

```

Actually, looking at the PR output again: `aav.aav.0x000113b0`. If the flag is created by `rz_flag_set` with name `aav.0x000113b0`, and then later another `aav` run finds the same address and creates another flag... but `rz_flag_set` would find the existing flag by name and return it. So the name wouldn't double.

Unless... the flag is being created in a different space. If the first `aav` run creates `aav.0x000113b0` in space "*", and the second `aav` run is in a different space, `rz_flag_get` would not find the flag by name (since `rz_flag_get` searches all spaces?), and `rz_flag_get_at` would not find it either (since it's in a different space), so it would create another `aav.0x000113b0` in the new space? No, `rz_flag_get` (by name) probably searches all spaces, while `rz_flag_get_at` (by offset) only searches the current space.

  1215	        if (!relocs) {
  1216	                relocs = o->relocs;
  1217	                if (!relocs) {
  1218	                        return false;
  1219	                }
  1220	        }
  1221	
  1222	        rz_flag_space_push(core->flags, RZ_FLAGS_FS_RELOCS);
  1223	
  1224	        Sdb *db = NULL;
  1225	        char *sdb_module = NULL;
  1226	        for (size_t i = 0; i < relocs->relocs_count; i++) {
  1227	                RzBinReloc *reloc = relocs->relocs[i];
  1228	                if (is_invalid_address_va(va, reloc->vaddr, reloc->paddr)) {
  1229	                        continue;
  1230	                }
  1231	                ut64 addr = rva(o, reloc->paddr, reloc->vaddr, va);
  1232	                if ((is_section_reloc(reloc) || is_file_reloc(reloc))) {
  1233	                        /*
  1234	                         * Skip section reloc because they will have their own flag.
  1235	                         * Skip also file reloc because not useful for now.
  1236	                         */
  1237	                        continue;
  1238	                }
  1239	                set_bin_relocs(core, o, reloc, va, &db, &sdb_module);
  1240	                ut64 meta_sz;
  1241	                if (meta_for_reloc(core, o, reloc, false, addr, &meta_sz)) {
  1242	                        rz_meta_set(core->analysis, RZ_META_TYPE_DATA, addr, meta_sz, NULL);
  1243	                }
  1244	                if (va && rz_bin_reloc_has_target(reloc) && meta_for_reloc(core, o, reloc, true, reloc->target_vaddr, &meta_sz)) {
  1245	                        rz_meta_set(core->analysis, RZ_META_TYPE_DATA, reloc->target_vaddr, meta_sz, NULL);
  1246	                }
  1247	        }
  1248	        RZ_FREE(sdb_module);
  1249	        sdb_free(db);
  1250	        rz_flag_space_pop(core->flags);
  1251	
  1252	        return relocs != NULL;
  1253	}
